title: Do countries owe athletes asylum? | ABC NEWS
description: Seven members of Iran's women's football team, who would likely have faced persecution if they returned home after their Women's Asian Cup campaign, had been granted asylum in Australia.   One of the Iranian women who sought asylum in Australia has changed her mind and decided to return home.   It isn't the first time a government has stepped in during a sport tournament amid global political tensions.   Reporting by Amal Wehbe, ABC News social media team. title: Drone SOS: Ukraine sends help to US, Arab states to combat Iranian drones | The World | ABC News
description: Ukraine is sending experts and interceptor drones to the Middle East to help the US and Gulf states defend against Iranian attacks. title: Grave fears for Iranian footballers after Australia asylum saga | The World | ABC News
description: The story of the six members of the Iranian women's football team gaining asylum in Australia reads like a political thriller - except this is real life.  On Monday night, five players slipped away from their minders on the Gold Coast and were then able to make their applications for asylum.   Two other members were granted asylum after staying in Brisbane before the team left for Sydney - one of them has since requested to return to Iran.   But there are grave concerns for the players who are returning to Iran, and for the families of those who didn't.   Tracey Holmes, journalist and the host of the Sports Ambassador podcast spoke to The World's Girish Sawlani about what the future holds for the Iranian players.  #ABCTheWorld #ABCAsiaPacific  Subscribe: http://ab.co/1svxLVE  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: Anne Hathaway's International Women's Day speech to the UN | ABC NEWS
description: Actress and UN Women Goodwill Ambassador Anne Hathaway joined leading voices on women's rights and justice for the official United Nations commemoration of International Women’s Day 2026.  Speaking at the event at United Nations Headquarters in New York, Hathaway said women's choosing to celebrate the day was not an accommodation of injustice, but instead affirmed a "determination to outlast it".  At a time when women's rights face renewed backlash around the world, the International Women’s Day 2026 event, held under the theme "Rights. Justice. Action. For All Women and Girls", underscored the power of women and girls, across generations, movements and cultures, in all their diversity, coming together to defend equality and demand change for all women and girls.
